The shared outcome broke Polymarket's winner-take-all negRisk structure. Resolution-layer failure, not an information gap.

market_question: Bitcoin above $105,000 on June 6?
analyst_note: BTC crossed above $105K in mid-to-late May 2025 and held into June on risk-on drift; Decrypt reported 'no immediately obvious reason for the latest leg up' beyond cooling trade-war fears. Ordinary movement crossing the threshold, not a discrete shock. (Exact June 6 close not independently pinned; classification rests on the absence of a catalyst.) Resolved YES.

market_question: US strikes Yemen by December 31?
analyst_note: US struck Yemen repeatedly through 2025 (Operation Rough Rider, 1,100+ strikes Mar-May; ceasefire May 6 then sporadic strikes), so 'strikes by Dec 31' = YES in reality. The strikes were ongoing and broadly expected, but the specific trigger and timing were not knowable in advance. Closer to a mispricing of a near-certainty than a clean information gap.

analyst_note: Solomon Labs MetaDAO public sale: commitments hovered at ~$3-5M for most of the Nov 15-18 2025 window, then spiked to $102,932,673 in the final hours (~50x target). A binary on a continuous quantity (total committed capital) that strands the confident side on a late spike, the same compression failure as the price brackets.

event_title: Will Pierre Poilievre lose his seat?
market_question: Will Pierre Poilievre lose his seat?
analyst_note: Pierre Poilievre lost his Carleton seat to Liberal Bruce Fanjoy in the Apr 28 2025 federal election, ending a ~20-year tenure (BBC, CBC). The national signal was public, Trump's return and his pressure on Canada reshaped the race and swung the country to the Liberals, but the riding-level outcome surprised the market, which had over-anchored on the incumbent. National information public; local swing unpriced.
